era_context: Early Bronze Age III. Egypt's Old Kingdom (Dynasty IV–V) is at its height. Mesopotamian city-states continue their competition for hegemony. The Indus Valley Civilization is in its Early Harappan phase. This period represents the apex of early state formation in the Old World.
geographic_coverage_gaps: East Asia beyond the Indus Valley
disconfirming_evidence: No events are documented for 2902 BCE, so no disconfirming evidence can be identified.
historiographic_note: The year 2902 BCE has no documented events in the surviving historical record. This reflects the general sparseness of year-by-year documentation in the third millennium BCE. The historical record is dominated by monumental inscriptions, king lists, and archaeological stratigraphy, which do not provide year-by-year granularity for most of the ancient world.
